header.banner: Introducing Achronyme — a language for zero-knowledge proofs. Read the announcement

hero.subtitle: A zero-knowledge language. Same syntax runs in a VM or compiles to arithmetic circuits.

sectionDual.title: Dual execution engine
sectionDual.subtitle: One language, two targets. No compromises.

featureVM.title: VM Mode
featureVM.desc: A real programming language with closures, recursion, mark-sweep GC, 37 opcodes, and 50 type methods. Build anything from binary search to cryptographic protocols.

featureCircuit.title: Circuit Mode
featureCircuit.desc: Same syntax compiles to R1CS or Plonkish constraints. Boolean propagation, constant folding, and taint analysis catch under-constrained bugs before proof generation.

sectionProofs.title: Proofs without the ceremony
sectionProofs.subtitle: Native Groth16 and PlonK provers. One binary, zero Node.js.

featureProve.title: First-Class Proofs
featureProve.desc: prove {} blocks return real proof objects. Chain them, extract components with proof_json(), verify inline with verify_proof(). Proofs are values, not CLI artifacts.

featureSolidity.title: On-Chain Verification
featureSolidity.desc: Export .r1cs and .wtns for snarkjs compatibility. Generate Solidity verifier contracts with --solidity. Deploy and verify proofs directly on Ethereum.

comparison.title: The difference
comparison.subtitle: Proving a Poseidon commitment: Circom + snarkjs vs Achronyme.

footer.tagline: A zero-knowledge DSL. Write code, generate proofs.
